summary: All acceptance criteria are met: unit tests cover deletion, idempotency, and file preservation; integration test verifies proxy remains operational after key deletion. Two major issues require attention before sign-off: significant duplication with the pre-existing test/test_ca_key_deletion.py (task explicitly warned against this), and fragile mitmproxy-internal API access in the integration test's shutdown helper.

suggestions.suggestion: 5 of 7 new unit tests duplicate coverage already present in test/test_ca_key_deletion.py. The task explicitly says to read that file first and avoid duplication. Only test_delete_ca_key_files_removes_private_key (all-3-together form) and test_delete_ca_key_files_partial are genuinely new. The rest — keeps_public_cert, idempotent, empty_dir, delete_ca_public_cert_removes_cert, delete_ca_public_cert_idempotent — map 1-to-1 to existing tests. Either move the two unique tests into the existing file and skip creating test/unit/test_delete_ca_key_files.py, or explicitly note which tests are consolidations rather than duplicates.
suggestions.benefit: Eliminates redundant test maintenance, prevents confusion about canonical test location, keeps test suite lean.

suggestions.suggestion: _shutdown_proxy accesses mitmproxy internals (addon.servers._instances) that are not part of the public API. This will break silently when mitmproxy updates internal structure — the hasattr guard means the alternative path (just master.shutdown()) always runs instead, making the extra code dead weight. Remove the inner Proxyserver block entirely and rely only on master.shutdown() + thread.join().
suggestions.benefit: Removes a coupling to mitmproxy internals that will break across versions; simplifies shutdown to 5 lines.

suggestions.suggestion: Integration test has no @pytest.mark.skipif(sys.platform != 'win32', reason='Windows only') despite the task acceptance criteria explicitly stating 'passes on Windows CI' (implying Linux skip). patterns.md confirms: 'Integration tests: real registry (Windows-only, skipif)'. Even if mitmproxy itself is cross-platform, CI intent per spec is Windows-gated.
suggestions.benefit: Aligns with project conventions and prevents unexpected failures on Linux CI runners.

suggestions.suggestion: loop_ref / master_ref dicts are a closure workaround. Use a single-element list or restructure the thread function with a dataclass/namedtuple for clarity. Exception: this is an acceptable pattern in Python < 3.x but nonlocal is cleaner in Python 3.12.
suggestions.benefit: Easier to follow thread data flow without mental model of dict-as-mutable-cell.

suggestions.suggestion: All except blocks in _shutdown_proxy are bare except Exception: pass. This silently hides shutdown errors, making test failures hard to diagnose. At minimum log the exception: except Exception as e: print(f'shutdown error: {e}').
suggestions.benefit: Faster debugging when integration test hangs or fails during teardown.
